rxn_class_summary: Anodic oxidative cyclization of tosyl-sulfonamide-tethered enol ethers, thioenol ethers, allylsilanes, and styrenes to form proline (pyrrolidine) derivatives via olefin radical cation intermediates trapped by the nitrogen nucleophile
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (RVC anode, Pt cathode) of the electron-rich olefin (enol ether/thioenol ether/styrene/allylsilane) to a one-electron olefin radical cation; LiOMe/n-BuLi deprotonates the sulfonamide N-H to enhance nucleophilicity for intramolecular trapping

key_intermediates.name: olefin radical cation of enol/thioenol ether substrate (6)
C-centered cyclized radical (7) after C-N bond formation
alpha-alkoxy/thio carbocation (8) formed by second anodic oxidation of 7
methoxylated pyrrolidine product (4) after MeOH trapping and deprotonation

electron_flow: Anode removes one electron from the electron-rich olefin to give a radical cation (resonance: olefin radical cation with N-anion, or N-centered radical with olefin); intramolecular C-N bond formation gives a C-centered radical (7), which is oxidized at the anode to a cation (8), then trapped by methanol to give the proline product.
rate_determining_step: Intramolecular cyclization of the radical cation by the deprotonated sulfonamide nitrogen (rate enhanced by LiOMe-mediated N-H deprotonation); under fast cyclization, methanol trapping of cation 8 must also be rapid to preserve yield

probe_reagents.name: methanol vs 30% methanol/THF solvent variation
probe_reagents.observation: Switching from MeOH to 30% MeOH/THF dropped yield of 4g from 83% to 28%, indicating that rapid trapping of cation 8 by MeOH is required after fast cyclization

probe_reagents.name: LiOMe (basic additive)
probe_reagents.observation: Improved cyclization yields by deprotonating the sulfonamide N-H, increasing N nucleophilicity

probe_reagents.name: less polar thioenol ether substrate (3b/3f)
probe_reagents.observation: Less polar radical cation gave higher yields, supporting a discrete radical cation intermediate

probe_reagents.name: lowered electrolyte concentration (0.03 M Et4NOTs)
probe_reagents.observation: Suppressed over-oxidation/elimination side path; raised yield of 4c to 50% and 4d to 73%

probe_reagents.name: allylsilane substrate 9a
probe_reagents.observation: Anodic oxidation of allylsilane initiated cyclization in 90% yield, consistent with radical cation generation on electron-rich olefin

probe_reagents.name: styrene substrate 9b
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation of styrene moiety triggered cyclization (88%), supporting generality of olefin radical cation mechanism

notes: Two resonance descriptions are given for intermediate 6: (a) olefin radical cation trapped by a nitrogen anion, or (b) a nitrogen-centered radical adding to the electron-rich olefin; the authors treat these as resonance forms rather than distinct mechanisms. Product 4g is formed as a single diastereomer, consistent with prior cyclizations using alcohol nucleophiles and ketene dithioacetals, suggesting a well-defined stereochemistry-determining cyclization transition state. No TEMPO/BHT trapping, KIE, or DFT studies are reported on these pages.
